author: @wizrom3046
text:
I would love to see you go full magnetic suspension, with no point contact on the end of the shaft.
You could replace the point contact with repelling magnets. Probably at both ends of the shaft to make it work well.
Or you might be able to do it with the main suspending magnets by reversing one side set so both sets push the shaft inwards AND suspend it... 👍

author: @iowasongs
text: I still am wondering why you can't just "hang" the needle, or pen down from the magnet vertically? Wouldn't you just need the single magnet then for the same result?

replies.author: @wizrom3046
replies.text:
It is less stable and the hanging shaft will swing around like a pendulum.
Robert's setup here uses gravity to hold the shaft more "firmly" against the suspending magnets. 🙂

replies.author: @iowasongs
replies.text: @wizrom3046 perhaps so? But it seems to me that you could use gravity to your advantage. Such as a weighted bottom. I remember watching my father spin a Philips head screwdriver hanging from a powerful magnet, the thing would spin forever......

author: @Obliv210
text: i have a question. can we take the mechanical advantage of the pulley, and somehow reverse the way it works? or something like that? im sure youve seen the multi mega ton hammers the huge steel warehouses use, well i was imagining running that machine with just pulleys, and the repeating pedal press of your foot, but delivering forging level power with each stroke. similarly the same concept to run a lathe

replies.text:
@chalichaligha3234  well I thought, you could easily build wind-generated or solar powered machines that simple rake all the desert  sand away into the ocean, or toc turn into bricks, along the nile, youve also got static electricity generated from all the sane!!! when i was a sand blaster, we always heard stories of guys getting shocked, and it was after theyd been sandblasting a PVC pipe, we could take the worlds recycled plastics, make small little table in the sand, so when the wind blows the sand over electricity is generated!  the point being we get all the sand out of the way and we can see what we have out there.    the other idea was, use wind, solar, etc, to build giant glaciers in the desert, you could use the water flowing away as rivers to create your hydro power, etc, but i thought of this idea while messing around with mining panning gold interests, everyone uses the rivers, and glacial flow etc, well if we could throw a mini glacier over a spot, you could technically, create an artificial water flow to allow you to search for gold in unsearched areas. thats all this world is! a big  hide bury and dig site, the world elites turning us to stone, melting the world, flooding the world etc! i do apologize for being all over the place.  

ALSO, you could effectively turn the work required to press a button into running the lathe, youve got to understand im talking about engineering multiple contraptions working in conjunction with one another to more or less have what we call a Perpetual Motion device. now ofc you will say they wont work etc they will fail on their own. YES CORRECT. INEVITABLY while A L O N E it will fail, but with the assistance of a human, say tapping their foot to a tune,(very light work) can be turned into a full scale, current modern giant steel lathes. you can turn the work of snapping your fingers into enough work to lift the pyramids if u like! oh let me show you this video as well you wil loooooooooove this.

author: @drydenstewartenterprises
text: I love all these videos involving magnetic bearings, as its something I am experimenting with when I have free time. Now if we were to go back to the old craddle where the magnetic repulsion is levitating the axle, instead of having the ball point pen or in this case the knitting needle, what if we had magnets on either end of the cradle and a magnet on each end of the axle repelling the magnets from the cradle, we shouldn't need any physical connection from the craddle to the axle. I believe there would be less friction (even though the friction from the pen or needle is quite small, it is there)  I just don't know if the magnetic field lines will interfere with each other and cause it to stop spinning.... I have a cradel 3d printed and ready for the magnets to be inserted, and an axle in production, but I just started a new job and don't have much time for my experiments right now.
